description_long: This bungalow features a single-story stone facade with a prominent front porch supported by two wooden posts. The roof is covered with reddish-brown ceramic tiles and is a classic gable style, sloping down on both sides. Lush green vines partially cover the right side of the building and extend onto the roof, adding a touch of nature to the structure. The front includes two large windows and a central door, while the rear has a single wooden door and a small utility panel. The side walls are mostly plain stone, with some vine growth and visible weathering. The overall appearance is rustic and inviting, suitable for a suburban or semi-rural neighborhood.
description: A single-story stone bungalow with a tiled gable roof, front porch, and green vines climbing the facade and roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the porch, two large windows, central door, and extensive vine growth on the right.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the gable roof, dense vine coverage, and plain stone wall.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single wooden door, small utility panel, and minimal vine presence.
description_view_3: Left side view displaying the gable roof, porch extension, and sparse vine growth.
